title: crataegus × bicknellii
intro: Crataegus × bicknellii, also known as Bicknell's hawthorn, is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the rose family, resulting from the cross between Crataegus monogyna and Crataegus crus-galli. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating cardiovascular issues due to its flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Crataegus × bicknellii include reducing blood pressure, improving lipid profiles, and enhancing cardiovascular function. The plant is commonly prepared into herbal teas, capsules, and extracts, which can be used to support heart health and alleviate symptoms of cardiovascular disease.
